title: Cyanotic Spells - Tetralogy of Fallot
chief_complaint: 4-month-old with episodes of sudden cyanosis and irritability, especially when crying. Parents note he squats during episodes. Known heart murmur since birth.

physical_exam.heart: Harsh systolic ejection murmur at left upper sternal border, single S2

hidden_data.echo: Large malaligned VSD, overriding aorta, RVOT obstruction (infundibular + valvular), RVH - classic TOF anatomy
hidden_data.cxr: Boot-shaped heart (coeur en sabot), decreased pulmonary vascular markings
hidden_data.ecg: Right ventricular hypertrophy, right axis deviation

correct_diagnosis: Tetralogy of Fallot with Hypercyanotic (Tet) Spells
key_learning_points: TOF = VSD + overriding aorta + RVOT obstruction + RVH
Tet spells: knee-chest position, oxygen, morphine, beta-blockers

patient_responses.spells: He turns blue when he cries, then squats and gets better

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! TOF with tet spells - this is a pediatric cardiac emergency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is Tetralogy of Fallot. The CXR + echo confirm the four components.
feedback.attending_note: Tet spell management: Knee-chest position, oxygen, morphine 0.1mg/kg SC/IM, propranolol 0.1mg/kg IV. Volume expansion. Surgical repair at 3-6 months. Avoid dehydration and iron deficiency. Excellent prognosis after repair.
